title: AI Rack Power Rearchitecture — 800V HVDC Analog Picks-and-Shovels
summary: AI racks are jumping from 120 kW to 600 kW–1 MW by 2027, forcing a wholesale switch from 54V to 800V HVDC distribution. TXN and ADI are the analog reference-design partners winning that re-architecture — and Seaport just tripled the analog TAM estimate today.
catalyst: On May 22, 2026, Seaport Research Partners upgraded Texas Instruments to Buy with a $400 price target, arguing the analog power-semiconductor TAM will expand from ~$5B today to $15B by 2030 as data centers re-architect from 54V to 800V HVDC distribution [1][2]. The upgrade lands two days after NVIDIA's Q1 FY27 print (May 20) — $81.6B revenue, $75.2B data center, $87B Q2 guide — which crystallized $725B of aggregate hyperscaler 2026 capex [3][4]. NVIDIA's Rubin Ultra 'Kyber' racks (600 kW baseline, scaling to ~1 MW, volume shipments late 2027) make the 800V transition non-optional: at 1 MW per rack, a 48V system would require roughly 450 lbs of copper per rack — physically infeasible [5][6]. Design wins are locking in now for 2028 mass deployment.
thesis: The market is paying up for generation picks-and-shovels (gas turbines, nuclear, grid contractors) but underpricing the analog chips inside the rack that make the 800V→6V→<1V conversion possible. TXN built the only complete NVIDIA-reference 800V VDC architecture, posted +90% YoY data-center revenue growth in Q1 2026 (now >$1B run-rate) and is raising prices on tight supply [7][8]. ADI announced a $1.5B all-cash deal for Empower Semiconductor (May 19) to plug the integrated-voltage-regulator gap and printed a record $3.62B Q2 with revenue +37% YoY and data-center/ATE on a 'steep growth trajectory' through 2027 [9][10]. Both are wide-moat, dividend-paying analogs trading at ~24-25x forward — quality compounders being repriced as AI infrastructure plays.
body_md:
## Setup

The binding constraint on AI compute has migrated downstream three times in eighteen months. First it was GPU supply (solved by Blackwell ramp). Then it was grid generation (covered in the AI Power Bottleneck thesis with GEV/CEG). Then it was transmission and switchgear (covered in the Data Center Grid Buildout thesis with PWR/ETN). The next constraint — already visible in NVIDIA's reference architectures — is *inside* the rack: getting megawatts of power from a building's 800V busbar down to the sub-1V rails that feed a Rubin GPU core.

At NVIDIA GTC 2026 (March 16-19), NVIDIA published its 800 VDC reference architecture as the only viable distribution standard for Rubin Ultra-class racks, which begin volume shipments late 2027 and ramp through 2028 [5][11]. The math is unforgiving: rack power density has gone from 40 kW (Hopper H100) to 120 kW (Blackwell GB200) to 600 kW baseline / ~1 MW peak (Rubin Ultra Kyber) — a 25x increase in three years [6]. At 1 MW per rack on 48V distribution, you would need ~450 pounds of copper per rack just for busbars; mechanically and thermally impossible at scale [5]. 800V HVDC reduces copper usage by 45%, eliminates up to four conversion stages, improves end-to-end efficiency by 5%, and lowers maintenance costs 70% [5][6].

This is not a marketing slide. It is a multi-year architectural commitment from NVIDIA's silicon partners — Texas Instruments, Analog Devices, Infineon, STMicro, Navitas, ON Semi, Power Integrations, ROHM and others — published as full reference designs validated against the Vera Rubin platform [11][12]. Design wins on the 800V architecture lock in supplier sockets for the 2028-2032 build cycle.

## Why Now

Three dated, verifiable events converged in the last 72 hours:

1. **NVDA Q1 FY27 (May 20, 2026):** Revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY), Data Center $75.2B (+92% YoY), Blackwell ~70% of compute revenue, Q2 guide $87B vs. Street $82-85B. NVDA also raised the buyback by $80B and bumped the dividend 25x from $0.01 to $0.25 per share [3][4]. The print confirms the $725B 2026 hyperscaler capex aggregate has not cracked — it is accelerating.
2. **Seaport upgrades TXN to Buy, $400 PT (May 22, 2026):** Analyst Jay Goldberg's note explicitly says the analog power-semi TAM expands from ~$5B to $15B by 2030 (3x), with margin profile improvements on top because the products serving 800V racks command higher ASPs [1][2]. TXN closed +4.69% at $312.21 [13].
3. **ADI announces Empower Semi acquisition (May 19, 2026):** $1.5B all-cash deal for IVR/silicon-capacitor technology that ADI's CEO described as filling 'critical gaps in our power portfolio to capitalize on AI infrastructure demands.' Empower's tech is claimed to cut data-center power consumption 10-15% [9].

The Seaport TAM math is the key unlock. The Street has historically modeled analog as a sleepy 6-8% revenue grower tethered to the industrial cycle. A 3x TAM expansion driven by a single architectural shift in the largest end-market (data center) is a re-rating event, not an earnings event.

## Company-Specific Edge

### Texas Instruments (TXN)

TI is the only analog supplier with a *complete* NVIDIA-validated 800 VDC reference design — a two-stage approach (800V→6V isolated bus converter at 97.6% peak efficiency and >2000 W/in³, then 6V→<1V multiphase buck) that fits the Kyber rack mechanical envelope [11][12]. The product line includes the 800V hot-swap controller, GaN-integrated bus converter, multiphase buck for GPU core power, plus a 30 kW 800V high-density AC/DC PSU and 800V capacitor bank units.

The financial signal: Q1 2026 revenue $4.8B (+19% YoY), with data center alone +90% YoY and now >$1B in annual run-rate [7][8]. Analog segment revenue +22% YoY. Management is raising prices on the data-center book because lead times are extended and supply is tight. Today's Seaport upgrade was the *fourth* Tier-2/Tier-3 Buy upgrade in three weeks — the analyst community is being dragged into the thesis.

IOWN screener gives TXN a 74 HOLD, but the underlying quality marks are striking: ROE 30.15%, profit margin 28.28%, AI Resilience 8/10, Infinite Game MIXED (7/10), 53 consecutive years of dividends paid. The 'HOLD' is largely a function of richness, not quality — TXN now trades at $312 vs. the $190 capture in the screener, but forward P/E of ~24x is in-line with prior cycle peaks despite a structurally higher data-center growth trajectory.

### Analog Devices (ADI)

ADI is the higher-quality cyclical recovery name with a sharper AI-specific catalyst. Q2 FY26 was a record: $3.62B revenue (+37% YoY, +15% sequential), EPS +67% YoY, gross margin 73%. Q3 guide $3.63B and Q4 EPS $3.10 — both above consensus [9][10]. Management called out data center and ATE businesses as on 'steep growth trajectories' with confidence building into 2027.

The Empower acquisition (announced May 19, $1.5B all-cash, closes 2H 2026) adds proprietary integrated voltage regulator (IVR) and silicon-capacitor technology. ADI was already a published NVIDIA 800 VDC partner with hot-swap and first-stage power solutions — Empower fills the IVR socket that sits closer to the GPU die [12]. The acquisition is small relative to ADI's $160B+ market cap but strategically dense.

IOWN screener: ADI scores 78 HOLD, with Infrastructure 9/10, AI Resilience 8/10, INFINITE Game mindset (8/10), 22 years of dividends paid and 21 grown. The screener flags an unusually low ROE (7.86%) — this reflects the Maxim acquisition goodwill weight on equity rather than operating returns, which are running at 30%+ on tangible capital. D/E of 25.75 is the cleanest in large-cap analog.

## Numbers That Matter

| Metric | TXN | ADI |
|---|---|---|
| Stock Price (May 22) | $312.21 [13] | up 3.81% on day [9] |
| Forward P/E (screener) | 24.07 | 24.83 |
| Most recent quarter rev growth | +19% YoY (Q1 FY26) | +37% YoY (Q2 FY26) |
| Data center YoY growth | ~+90% | 'steep' / not disclosed |
| Gross / profit margin | 28% net | 73% gross |
| ROE | 30.15% | 7.86% (goodwill drag) |
| AI Resilience (IOWN) | 8/10 | 8/10 |
| Dividend track | 53 yrs paid | 22 yrs paid, 21 grown |
| Infinite Game (IOWN) | MIXED (7/10) | INFINITE (8/10) |
| Recent M&A | — | Empower Semi, $1.5B (May 19) |

Analog TAM math (Seaport, May 22): ~$5B today → $15B by 2030 = ~25% CAGR, with margin expansion on top.

## Bear Case

**The whole AI capex narrative cracks.** NVDA Q1 was a beat but the bar keeps moving. If Q2 or Q3 misses the $87B+ trajectory, the data-center analog re-rating compresses fastest in TXN (highest beta within the duo).

**800V deployment slips.** Seaport's own note flags that volume Kyber shipments are 'late 2027' — there is a ~18-month gap between today's design wins and actual revenue. If Rubin Ultra slips to 2028 or AI factory construction is paced by power availability (a real risk per the AI Power Bottleneck thesis), data-center analog revenue lags the narrative by 2-3 quarters.

**Competition compresses the TAM share.** STMicro, Infineon, ON Semi and Navitas are all NVIDIA reference partners [12]. The $15B TAM by 2030 will be sliced — TXN and ADI are best-positioned but not exclusive. ON Semi's silicon-carbide platform is competitive in 1200V-class rack distribution.

**TXN payout pressure.** The screener flags a TXN dividend payout ratio at 100.92% — a near-term constraint on dividend growth if FCF doesn't ramp on the data-center mix shift. Not a thesis breaker but a quality flag.

**Margin pressure from GaN cost.** GaN power stages are still expensive vs. silicon at scale. If GaN cost curves don't fall, the BOM math on 800V conversion gets harder and the architecture transition stretches further.

## Conclusion

The consensus AI infrastructure trade has been GPUs → gas turbines → switchgear. The next leg — analog chips inside the rack — has its first major Wall Street validation today (Seaport, $15B TAM by 2030). NVIDIA has published a hard architectural commitment to 800V HVDC, with Rubin Ultra Kyber racks beginning volume in late 2027. TXN owns the most complete reference design; ADI just bought the missing IVR piece. Both are quality compounders with 20-50+ year dividend track records, IOWN AI Resilience scores of 8/10, and forward multiples of ~24-25x — high but not stretched relative to a structurally higher growth trajectory. The setup is asymmetric because the design-win window is now and the revenue payoff is 18-30 months out — exactly the kind of asymmetry the market consistently underprices.

ticker_rationale.TXN: Only analog supplier with a complete NVIDIA-validated 800V VDC reference design (97.6% bus-converter efficiency, >2000 W/in³ density). Q1 2026 data center revenue +90% YoY, >$1B run-rate, raising prices. Seaport's $400 PT today implies ~28% upside from $312. IOWN screener: 74 HOLD, AI Resilience 8/10, ROE 30.15%, 53 years of dividends.
ticker_rationale.ADI: Record Q2 FY26: $3.62B revenue (+37% YoY), 73% gross margin, data-center/ATE 'steep growth trajectory' through 2027. $1.5B Empower Semi acquisition (May 19) plugs the IVR gap directly inside the 800V→core rail. IOWN screener: 78 HOLD, INFINITE Game (8/10), Infrastructure 9/10, AI Resilience 8/10, 22 years dividends paid / 21 grown.

risks: NVDA Q2 FY27 (August 2026) or Q3 FY27 miss against the $87B+ trajectory — would compress the entire data-center supply chain, with TXN's now-stretched multiple most exposed.
Rubin Ultra Kyber rack volume shipments slip from late 2027 into 2028+ — design-win revenue would lag the narrative by 2-3 quarters and create a multi-quarter dead zone for the trade.
Competitive TAM share compression — STMicro, Infineon, ON Semi, Navitas, ROHM are all NVIDIA 800V reference partners. The $15B 2030 TAM will be sliced; TXN/ADI are best-positioned but not exclusive.
TXN dividend payout ratio sits at 100.92% in the screener — limited room for dividend growth without FCF acceleration, and any data-center mix-shift delay tightens the constraint.
GaN power-stage cost curves don't fall fast enough — would stretch the 800V transition timeline as hyperscalers stick with hybrid 48V/800V topologies for longer than expected.
invalidation: TXN data-center revenue growth decelerates below +50% YoY in any quarter (currently +90%) — would signal the architectural design-win cycle is not converting to revenue on schedule.
ADI data-center commentary downgrades from 'steep growth trajectory' to 'softening' on any subsequent earnings call — direct management signal the AI sub-segment is rolling over.
NVIDIA officially delays Rubin Ultra Kyber volume from late 2027 to 2H 2028 or later — pushes the revenue payoff out by 12+ months and breaks the time-frame of this thesis.
Seaport's $15B 2030 analog TAM is revised down by any major sell-side house (Morgan Stanley, Bernstein, Bank of America) within 6 months — would suggest the architectural-shift TAM math is contested.
TXN forward P/E expands above 32x or ADI above 30x with no acceleration in data-center growth — would suggest narrative-driven multiple expansion has gone too far ahead of fundamentals.
